pre_existing_findings.finding: Contains Chinese text: 必须使用正常的UI流程创建session，永远不允许使用代码创建session！
pre_existing_findings.baseline_note: The CJK line is present in the task pre-diff baseline (13fef0f, line 1044) and is unchanged by this task; current line number shifted to hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh:1235 due to surrounding insertions in this diff. Therefore it is not a new AC-2.6(b) finding.
pre_existing_findings.recommendation: Translate to English and add task-id attribution per exemption policy

agents/changelog-analyst.md.changed_lines: 42-43 (DO NOT items 13-14: two text prose lines added)
agents/changelog-analyst.md.standard_1: No new inline code blocks added in this diff. Pre-existing bash blocks throughout the file are behavioral workflow instructions to the LLM agent (standard pattern for agent spec files), not scripts that should be extracted. Changed lines 42-43 are plain text. No new Standard 1 finding.
agents/changelog-analyst.md.standard_8: Added lines are concise DO NOT prohibitions with no marketing language or verbose prose.
agents/changelog-analyst.md.standard_10: Reference to `${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/scripts/resolve-dev-report.py` at line 144 resolves to an existing file. No dangling-reference violation.

hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh.changed_lines: Layer 1.F block replaced: old grep-qE regex entry gate removed, new grep -F substring matches added; Python shlex helper replaced bash compound-detection; M5 canonical form check added at line 679
hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh.standard_2: Changed hook lines add protected-path/security-pattern constants (`/tmp/claude-bulk-*`, `write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py`, `/root/.claude/scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py`). These are literal guard targets required for security matching and are not runtime-configurable values, so no Standard 2 violation.
hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh.standard_3: No new direct `python3` calls without venv activation in changed lines. The Python heredoc invocations use `$PYTHON_BIN` which is resolved from the venv at script start.
hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh.standard_6_cjk: CJK text at line 1235 is pre-existing (present in baseline 13fef0f at line 1044). Not in the diff hunk. Not a new AC-2.6(b) finding.
hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh.standard_9: Hook receives input via stdin JSON as its parameterization interface — the expected interface for PreToolUse hooks.
hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh.standard_10: New reference to `write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py` in comments at lines 672, 678, 914 resolves to `scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py` (confirmed to exist). No dangling-reference violation.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex returned 4 wording-precision issues and 1 OBSERVATION_ONLY. No missed critical violations. All 4 issues had PROPOSED_FIX. Codex confirmed zero new AC-2.6(b) violations in this diff.

findings.issue: Standard 6 baseline reference should name the specific task pre-diff SHA (13fef0f) rather than HEAD~1, since HEAD~1 is not the authoritative task baseline.
findings.proposed_fix: Replace HEAD~1 scoping with: 'The CJK line is present in the task pre-diff baseline (13fef0f, line 1044) and is unchanged by this task; current line number shifted to hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh:1235 due to later insertions. Therefore it is not a new AC-2.6(b) finding.'
findings.rationale: Correct — the task baseline is the commit the task started from (13fef0f / HEAD at task-start), not HEAD~1 which could be a different parent depending on when the diff is taken. Applied to baseline_note in pre_existing_findings.

findings.issue: Standard 2 clean rationale cited api.life-ai.app URLs (pre-existing in the file) rather than the security-pattern constants that are actually the changed lines (protected-path prefixes and write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py references).
findings.proposed_fix: Replace with: 'Changed hook lines add protected-path/security-pattern constants (`/tmp/claude-bulk-*`, `write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py`, `/root/.claude/scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py`). These are literal guard targets required for security matching and are not runtime-configurable values, so no Standard 2 violation.'
findings.rationale: Correct — the changed lines are the Layer 1.F block replacements which contain new path pattern constants, not the pre-existing URL patterns. Applied to per_file_notes.hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh.standard_2.

findings.issue: Standard 10 clean rationale only mentioned resolve-dev-report.py but the diff also adds references to write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py in comments of pretool-bash-safety.sh.
findings.proposed_fix: Replace with: 'New reference `write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py` resolves to `scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py`; existing `resolve-dev-report.py` references also resolve. No dangling-reference violation.'
findings.rationale: Correct — write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py is referenced in the new hook comments and should be explicitly confirmed as existing (it does exist). Applied to per_file_notes.

findings.issue: Standard 1 clean rationale should be clearer that the diff adds only DO NOT prose at agents/changelog-analyst.md:42-43 and does not add or modify code blocks, and that pre-existing code-block candidates are outside the changed-line range.
findings.proposed_fix: Replace with: 'No Standard 1 new finding: this diff adds only DO NOT prose at agents/changelog-analyst.md:42-43 and does not add or modify code blocks. Any pre-existing code-block candidates are outside the changed-line range and non-blocking under AC-2.6(b).'

findings.issue: OBSERVATION_ONLY: Codex did not find any missed critical new violation in the scoped changed lines. The draft's final conclusion — zero new AC-2.6(b) violations — appears correct after the wording fixes.
findings.rationale: No action needed. Confirms correctness of zero-violation verdict.
